name: Stack Auth API
what_it_does: Open-source authentication and user management platform with REST API — built for Next.js with multi-tenant support, OAuth, magic links, MFA, teams, and user data management, offering self-hosted or managed cloud deployment.
use_cases: Adding authentication to Next.js apps with deep framework integration via SDK
Managing users, teams, and permissions programmatically via Management API
Self-hosting a full auth stack with multi-tenant team support
Implementing OAuth social login without building OAuth infrastructure from scratch
Migrating from Auth0 or Clerk with an open-source alternative that can be self-hosted
not_for: Non-Next.js stacks needing deep framework integration (consider Kinde or Auth0)
Enterprise identity governance with SCIM, SIEM integration, and compliance reporting
Teams without engineering capacity to maintain self-hosted auth infrastructure
best_when: A Next.js team wants open-source authentication with self-hosting option, team management, and an escape hatch from vendor lock-in.
avoid_when: You need proven enterprise identity management, non-Next.js framework integration, or zero operational overhead.

auth.notes: Project API key for server-side Management API. User access tokens (short-lived JWT) for client auth. Refresh token rotation. Scopes for user and team resource access. Secret API key for server operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited users on self-hosted; cloud free up to 10,000 MAU
pricing.notes: MIT licensed open source — completely free to self-host. Cloud managed option available. YC-backed (W24). Focus on Next.js ecosystem. Growing rapidly as Clerk alternative with self-hosting option. Strong GitHub presence.

performance.rate_limits: Standard rate limits on cloud; unlimited on self-hosted

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers users, teams, permissions, and OAuth connections. JWT validation for user sessions. Webhook support for auth events. Next.js SDK with server component support. MIT open source — can inspect and extend. Active development with frequent releases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation idempotent by email. Team creation idempotent. Permission assignments idempotent. Token issuance creates new tokens. OAuth connections idempotent per provider.
known_agent_gotchas: Primarily optimized for Next.js — other framework SDKs less mature
Early-stage product — API may evolve; pin SDK versions for stability
Self-hosted requires database setup (PostgreSQL) and Redis for sessions
Team-based access control requires careful team context in API calls

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open source — fully auditable. SOC2 in progress (cloud). GDPR compliant. JWT with refresh rotation limits exposure. Self-hosted gives full data control. OAuth provider connections use secure PKCE flow. YC-backed with security-conscious team.
